executive_summary: Trump's declaration that the Iran war is 'close to over' sends global markets to record highs while policymakers warn investors are underestimating war's economic toll. Pro-Russian cyberattacks target Swedish critical infrastructure, highlighting escalating hybrid warfare threats across Europe.

key_insights.insight: Trump declares Iran war 'close to over' as S&P 500 hits record high and oil markets remain volatile
Policymakers warn markets are underestimating the economic toll of ongoing global conflicts
Swedish power plant targeted by pro-Russian cyber/physical attack in 2025
US launches probe into suspicious oil trades made before Trump's policy pivots
Public sentiment souring on AI and data centers as Anthropic and OpenAI pursue IPOs
China's BYD reports fire incident at Shenzhen garage for test and scrapped vehicles
Silver market facing sixth consecutive annual deficit
El Salvador enacts law allowing life sentences for minors as young as 12
BofA stock traders achieve record performance driven by market momentum
Mark Mobius, pioneering emerging markets investor, passes away at 89

blind_spot: Rising anti-tech sentiment and regulatory pushback against AI/data center expansion in Western democracies represents a shifting political landscape that could reshape tech-industry geopolitics and transatlantic regulatory alignment.
